subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Leading vs Lagging Indicators
description: A short lesson to help you apply Leading vs Lagging Indicators.

hook.text: Counting injuries tells you what went wrong. Counting safety walks tells you what will go right.

buildup.text: Lagging indicators—injury rates, lost time—measure past failures after they've happened.

discovery.text: Leading indicators—training hours, inspections, near-miss reports—predict future safety.

twist.text: A company with zero injuries this year might have terrible leading indicators—disaster is building.

climax.text: Measuring both types gives a complete picture of safety health.

punchline.text: Don't wait for someone to get hurt to measure safety. Count the prevention.

quiz.question: What is an example of a leading safety indicator?
